books.title: Making Your Own Maps
books.author: Susan Hansen
books.rationale: This book provides great examples of different kinds of maps that students will be creating in their geography lessons.

books.title: Redwoods
books.author: Jason Chin
books.rationale: This book about the redwood trees interweaves a story about a boy traveling to the forest and includes facts about these massive trees.

books.title: Cross Country: Geography for Children
books.author: Paul Hanna, Clyde Kohn
books.rationale: Travel from Los Angeles to Washington, D.C. with Tom, Ruth, and their mother and father. As they encounter the different regions of the United States, the children learn about a variety of subjects, including basic geography concepts (landforms, waterbodies, and beginner map skills), as well as about such topics as local economies, climate, and regional plants and animals.

books.title: 50 Adventures in the 50 States
books.author: Kate Siber
books.rationale: This beautifully illustrated book provides a two-page spread for each state and shows what activities can be done based on the geographical features of the area. This book will be used alongside Cross Country to follow the Page family as they travel across the country.

books.title: United States Atlas 7th Ed.
books.author: National Geographic Kids
books.rationale: This atlas is used across the curriculum as a reference and as a text to read in some lessons. With interesting information and pictures, this is an economical reference for students.
